strengths.detail: The organization directs <cite id="5">100.0% of its spending toward programs</cite>, ensuring that donor contributions are not diverted to administrative overhead.
Najam Institute reports <cite id="6">$0.00 in fundraising expenses</cite> per dollar raised, a rare level of efficiency for a growing nonprofit.

strengths_deep_dive: The Najam Institute Foundation demonstrates a high degree of financial leaness, as evidenced by its <cite id="9">1.0 months of working capital</cite>, which provides a stable buffer for its operations. Its growth trajectory is notable, with a <cite id="10">3-year revenue CAGR of 373.0%</cite>, reflecting rapidly increasing donor trust and expanded program reach. The organization's commitment to Islamic scholarship is formalized through a <cite id="11">documented theory of change</cite>, which bridges the gap between classical religious education and modern humanitarian needs.
rich_narrative.summary: Based in Decatur, Georgia, the Najam Institute Foundation Inc is a multifaceted nonprofit dedicated to <cite id="1">providing humanitarian help to families in need</cite> while offering <cite id="2">accessible classical Islamic education</cite> through its specialized academic programs. The organization operates several key initiatives, including the <cite id="3">Najam Seminary and Najam Academy</cite>, which provide flexible learning schedules for students of Islamic knowledge. In the 2023 fiscal year, the foundation experienced significant growth, reporting <cite id="4">total revenue of $1,181,926</cite>, a substantial increase from previous years. A defining characteristic of the organization's financial model is its <cite id="5">100.0% program expense ratio</cite>, indicating that all functional expenses are directed toward its core mission of education and relief. Furthermore, the institute maintains <cite id="6">exceptional fundraising efficiency</cite>, spending $0.00 to raise every $1 of contribution income. Beyond its educational scope, the foundation acts as a significant benefactor to other charitable causes, distributing <cite id="7">$774,261 in total grants</cite> during the most recent tax year. These grants support both <cite id="8">domestic community centers</cite> and international relief efforts, reinforcing its dual mission of scholarship and service.
